The new window-option support adds useful coverage, but the current implementation does not preserve tmux-style option inheritance semantics for window-scoped options.\\n\\n### Stage 2 — Code Quality / Verification\\n- `zls` diagnostics: **no diagnostics** on all modified Zig files (`src/main.zig`, `src/server.zig`, `src/cmd/cmd.zig`, `src/cmd/config_shell_test.zig`, `src/cmd/option_commands_test.zig`, `src/keybind/bindings.zig`, `src/config/path.zig`)\\n- Targeted pattern scan: no obvious debug-print / empty-catch / hardcoded-secret hits in the modified source set\\n- Verification commands passed:\\n  - `zig build test`\\n  - `zig build`\\n  - `git diff --check`\\n\\n### Issues\\n\\n[MEDIUM] Window-option unset ignores global inheritance and resets to baked-in defaults  \\n**File:** `src/cmd/cmd.zig:605-615`, `src/cmd/cmd.zig:3398-3413`  \\n**Issue:** `restoreDefaultWindowOption()` hardcodes `\\\"emacs\\\"`, `\\\"#I:#W#F\\\"`, and `false` booleans, and `cmdSetOption(... -u ...)` calls that helper for per-window unsets. That means a sequence like `set -g mode-keys vi`, `setw mode-keys emacs`, `setw -u mode-keys` will resolve to `\\\"emacs\\\"` instead of inheriting the current global window default (`\\\"vi\\\"`). This contradicts the repo’s own scoped-inheritance model for options.  \\n**Fix:** Make per-window unset restore from `server.window_defaults` or, better, route window options through the existing scoped option store so unsetting a window option re-exposes the global value. Add a regression test for `setw -u` after a global window-option change.\\n\\n[MEDIUM] Global window-option updates overwrite explicit per-window overrides  \\n**File:** `src/cmd/cmd.zig:617-639`, `src/cmd/cmd.zig:658-661`  \\n**Issue:** `applyGlobalWindowOption()` and `restoreGlobalWindowOption()` iterate every live window and write the new value directly into each one. Because the runtime now stores only concrete values on each window, a later `set -g` silently destroys any explicit per-window override created by `setw`. That is not tmux-compatible behavior for global-vs-local window scopes.  \\n**Fix:** Preserve override state instead of flattening everything into concrete window values. Practically, that means either:\\n1. track whether each window option is inherited vs locally overridden, and only fan out global changes to inheriting windows, or  \\n2. migrate window-option reads/writes onto the existing scoped option store so inheritance is resolved at read time rather than by destructive copying.\\n\\n### Recommendation\\n**REQUEST CHANGES**\\n\\n\"}}\n</subagent_notification>","trigger_turn":false}

{"author":"/root/arch_validation","recipient":"/root","other_recipients":[],"content":"<subagent_notification>\n{\"agent_path\":\"/root/arch_validation\",\"status\":{\"completed\":\"## Summary\\nApproved, with one non-blocking architectural caveat. The diff materially improves common tmux compatibility by adding startup fallback from `~/.config/zmux/zmux.conf` to `~/.tmux.conf`, teaching `source-file` to expand `~/...`, wiring `C-b C-b` to `send-prefix`, and adding real window-option handling plus persistence for future windows. The main limitation is semantic rather than blocking: global window-option changes are implemented by eagerly overwriting every existing window, not by tracking inherited-vs-local option provenance.\\n\\n## Analysis\\n- **Config-path compatibility is implemented coherently across runtime, command path handling, tests, and docs.**\\n  - New `expandHomePath` expands only a leading `~/` and otherwise leaves paths unchanged, which is the right narrow helper for tmux-style config paths. `src/config/path.zig:3-12`\\n  - `Server.loadConfigFile` now expands `~/...`, returns a success flag when a file was actually opened, and `loadDefaultConfig` falls back from `~/.config/zmux/zmux.conf` to `~/.tmux.conf`. `src/server.zig:261-316`\\n  - `cmd source-file` uses the same helper before `open(2)`, so interactive/manual sourcing matches startup behavior. `src/cmd/cmd.zig:3571-3585`\\n  - The new coverage exercises both startup fallback and `source-file ~/.tmux.conf`. `src/server.zig:1375-1431`, `src/cmd/config_shell_test.zig:186-246`\\n  - README wording was updated to match the new behavior. `README.md:20-21`, `README.md:214-215`\\n\\n- **The default keybinding change is small and correct.**\\n  - `BindingManager.setupDefaults()` now binds prefix-table `C-b` to `send-prefix`, which is the standard tmux “prefix twice sends prefix” behavior. `src/keybind/bindings.zig:177-219`\\n  - That binding is backed by an existing handler that writes the configured prefix byte to the active pane. `src/cmd/cmd.zig:2949-2969`\\n  - Both the binding path and handler path are covered by tests. `src/keybind/bindings.zig:256-265`, `src/cmd/cmd.zig:5270-5305`\\n\\n- **Window-option support is now routed through the command layer in a way that fits the current brownfield design.**\\n  - `cmd.zig` adds a small allowlist for supported window options and centralizes apply/show/restore helpers for `mode-keys`, `window-status-format`, `aggressive-resize`, and `remain-on-exit`. `src/cmd/cmd.zig:578-716`\\n  - `set-window-option` and `show-window-options` are clean wrappers that forward to the main option handlers with `-w`, instead of duplicating parsing logic. `src/cmd/cmd.zig:2085-2094`, `src/cmd/cmd.zig:2227-2235`\\n  - `cmdSetOption`/`cmdShowOptions` now understand those window options, including `-g`, `-u`, quiet mode, and value-only display. `src/cmd/cmd.zig:2096-2153`, `src/cmd/cmd.zig:3398-3449`\\n  - Tests cover active-window mutation, value-only output, and future-window defaults. `src/cmd/option_commands_test.zig:181-245`\\n\\n- **Persisting window defaults in `Server` is internally consistent with how new windows are created.**\\n  - The server now owns `window_defaults` and can copy them into new windows through `applyWindowDefaults`. `src/server.zig:61-91`, `src/server.zig:173-181`\\n  - That copy is applied in session creation and in command paths that allocate new windows (`break-pane`, `new-window`, `link-window`). `src/server.zig:356-358`, `src/cmd/cmd.zig:1337-1340`, `src/cmd/cmd.zig:2557-2560`, `src/cmd/cmd.zig:4899-4902`\\n  - `main.zig` correctly exports the new config helper and includes it in the test root. `src/main.zig:39-43`, `src/main.zig:366-369`\\n\\n## Root Cause\\nThe only real architectural tension is that **window options are modeled as concrete fields on `Window.Options`, not as inherited state with provenance**. `Window.init()` allocates concrete default strings directly into each window, and setters overwrite those concrete fields in place. `src/window.zig:133-172`, `src/window.zig:409-418`  \\nBecause of that design, the new global window-option support has to copy server defaults into windows and later mutate every existing window when `-g` changes. `src/server.zig:173-181`, `src/cmd/cmd.zig:617-663`  \\nThat is consistent with the current brownfield architecture, but it means the implementation cannot distinguish “this window inherited the global default” from “this window was explicitly overridden locally.”\\n\\n## Recommendations\\n1. **Approve as-is** — **low effort** — **high impact**  \\n   The current changes are coherent, tested, and improve everyday tmux compatibility in exactly the touched areas.\\n\\n2. **Document the window-option limitation for future parity work** — **low effort** — **medium impact**  \\n   Before adding more window-scoped options, note that global window-option updates currently overwrite concrete per-window state because there is no inheritance/provenance layer. The hot spots are `applyGlobalWindowOption` / `restoreGlobalWindowOption` and `Window.Options`. `src/cmd/cmd.zig:617-663`, `src/window.zig:133-172`\\n\\n3. **If full tmux inheritance semantics become a goal, move window options onto a layered store instead of eager-copy mutation** — **medium/high effort** — **high impact**  \\n   That would avoid global updates stomping explicit local window overrides, but it is a larger architectural change and not necessary for this diff.\\n\\n## Trade-offs\\n| Option | Pros | Cons |\\n|--------|------|------|\\n| **A. Current approach: server defaults + eager copy/mutation** | Small diff, aligns with existing structs and command flow, easy to test | Cannot represent inherited-vs-local window-option provenance |\\n| **B. True inherited window-option model** | Closer tmux semantics, cleaner future expansion for window-scoped options | Larger refactor across `Window`, command handlers, and option resolution paths |\\n\\n## Verification\\n- `zig build test` ✅\\n- `zig build` ✅\\n- `git diff --check -- README.md src/main.zig src/server.zig src/cmd/cmd.zig src/cmd/config_shell_test.zig src/cmd/option_commands_test.zig src/keybind/bindings.zig src/config/path.zig` ✅\\n\\n## Confidence\\n- Cost-effectiveness: **0.98**\\n- Scalability: **0.91**\\n- Reliability: **0.95**\\n\\n## References\\n- `src/config/path.zig:3-12` - narrow `~/` expansion helper\\n- `src/server.zig:261-316` - startup config loading with legacy fallback\\n- `src/cmd/cmd.zig:3571-3585` - `source-file` now expands `~/...`\\n- `src/keybind/bindings.zig:177-219` - default `C-b C-b -> send-prefix` binding\\n- `src/cmd/cmd.zig:2949-2969` - `send-prefix` implementation\\n- `src/cmd/cmd.zig:578-716` - window-option apply/show helpers\\n- `src/cmd/cmd.zig:2085-2153` - `set/show-window-options` routing\\n- `src/cmd/cmd.zig:3398-3449` - window-option set/unset handling\\n- `src/server.zig:61-91` - server-owned `window_defaults`\\n- `src/server.zig:173-181` - copy defaults into newly created windows\\n- `src/window.zig:133-172` - concrete per-window option storage\\n- `src/cmd/option_commands_test.zig:181-245` - command-level coverage for window options\\n- `src/cmd/config_shell_test.zig:186-246` - `source-file ~/.tmux.conf` coverage\\n- `src/server.zig:1375-1431` - startup fallback coverage\\n- `README.md:20-21` - feature summary updated\\n- `README.md:214-215` - configuration fallback documented\"}}\n</subagent_notification>","trigger_turn":false}
